title: mariscus boreochrysocephalus
side_effects: Mariscus boreochrysocephalus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high toxicity levels. Ingestion of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as hives and itching, in some individuals. Furthermore, improper use of Mariscus boreochrysocephalus can cause liver damage and kidney problems, especially in people with pre-existing medical conditions. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to neurological symptoms, including headaches and dizziness.
